description: Sustained study or inquiry can become someone's work. That person is 'thonua', whether or not an institution employs or credentials them. Scholarship does not make its practitioner wise or correct. A 'thunai' (student) is presently learning, while a 'phewo' (sage) is regarded as having wisdom shaped through experience, reflection, or practice.
articulatory_notes: Rest the tongue tip lightly against the upper teeth for unvoiced 'th', let air pass along it, and move into true-mid back rounded 'o'. Touch the tongue tip to the backs of the upper teeth for dental 'n' and carry nasal voice into stressed high-back rounded 'u'. Keep the final vowel separate across the hiatus and open into central unrounded 'a'.
usage_notes: This is a role noun; 'theo' (read), 'somela' (observe), 'shonela' (learn), and other verbs state the work being done. Academic ranks, appointments, credentials, affiliations, and fields retain the source terms that establish them.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Sustained inquiry has lived in workshops, gardens, observatories, monasteries, libraries, and oral traditions as well as in modern institutions. The role follows the work rather than one setting.

semantic_domains.cognition: a person engaged in sustained study or inquiry
semantic_domains.community: a recognised role centred on scholarly work
